Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First we need the ordered activity sequence per case id sorted by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: DuckDB has list agg which can take an order by clause list agg activity order by timestamp would give the sequence as a list thats the variant key

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we need to group by that variant to count how many cases each has Then get the top K by count Then filter the original event log to only include cases that are in those top K variants

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets use a parameter say top k so the user can replace it with whatever K they want

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First step create a CTE that has each case id and its variant SELECT case id list agg activity order by timestamp AS variant sequence FROM event log GROUP BY case id

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: What if there are duplicate timestamps for the same case maybe we should add a tiebreaker but the problem does not mention it so proceed as per the problem statement

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then next we need to count the number of cases per variant another CTE variant counts which groups by variant sequence counts the case ids and uses row number over order by case count desc as variant rank

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets structure it with CTEs for clarity

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Maybe use a string aggregation instead like string agg activity order by timestamp as variant string that might be easier to group by but list agg is also fine since DuckDB compares lists element wise

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Outline the steps again For each case compute its activity sequence ordered by timestamp case variants Aggregate case variants by sequence to get count per variant rank variants by count descending variant rank Filter variant rank to keep only top K top k variants Join top k variants back to case variants to get all case ids that are in top K variants top k cases Join top k cases with the original event log to get all events for those cases

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If K is larger than the number of unique variants then row number would just return all which is fine

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If two variants have the same count row number would arbitrarily rank them but the problem says top K variants so row number is probably okay though if ties need to be handled use dense rank

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The query with case variants variant counts top k variants top k cases and final join to event log makes sense

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Another approach first get the top K variant sequences then find all case ids that have those sequences then select from event log where case id is in that list

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: Alternative query with case variants and variant metadata using count over partition by variant sequence and row number over order by count over partition by variant sequence desc

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Lets see if that works

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: That is a problem because if we do row number over the case variants rows which are per case then the rank is per case not per variant so that approach wont work

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we need to first aggregate to the variant level to get the rank then join back to get the cases So the first approach is right

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: If we want to make the variant a string for readability we can use string agg instead of list agg

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final output should be the original event log rows filtered to the top K cases so select el all is correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Sample test with variants AB count 3 and AC count 1 shows that top K equal 1 returns all events for cases 1 2 and 4 which is correct

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If ties for the Kth position need to be included replace row number with dense rank in the variant counts CTE

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If there are multiple events with the same timestamp in a case the order is undefined so add a secondary sort key like activity or an event id if available
